https://openalex.org/keywords/environmen.question_title: Does environmental quality matter for GDP through energy use or renewable adoption?
https://openalex.org/keywords/environmen.short_why: The nearest support graph keeps returning renewable energy, energy use, and the environmental Kuznets channel as the main routes linking environmental quality to output.
https://openalex.org/keywords/environmen.first_next_step: Start with papers that jointly study environmental quality, GDP, and one of the candidate channels, then check which channel is treated as an intermediate outcome rather than a parallel correlate.

https://openalex.org/keywords/r-d-intens.question_title: Does R&D intensity raise growth through innovation effort, high-tech exports, or tax policy?
https://openalex.org/keywords/r-d-intens.short_why: This card is strongest when the mechanism is treated as how research intensity becomes growth, not just whether the two move together.
https://openalex.org/keywords/r-d-intens.first_next_step: Separate papers that use innovation effort, high-tech export exposure, or tax policy as the intermediate margin, then ask which one survives in a common empirical frame.

https://openalex.org/keywords/digital-tr.question_title: Does digital transformation raise productivity through innovation, product quality, or absorptive capacity?
https://openalex.org/keywords/digital-tr.short_why: The local graph points to a tight mechanism story: digital adoption seems to matter when it changes firms' innovation margins, quality, or ability to absorb new technology.
https://openalex.org/keywords/digital-tr.first_next_step: Start with firm-level papers that measure digital adoption and at least one of the three channels, then compare whether the productivity effect attenuates once the channel is included.

jel:E25:Wage__jel:D24:Productivity.question_title: Do wages raise productivity through employment margins, firm scale, or offshoring responses?
jel:E25:Wage__jel:D24:Productivity.short_why: This is richer than a simple wage-productivity link because the nearby literature suggests distinct adjustment margins rather than one reduced-form effect.
jel:E25:Wage__jel:D24:Productivity.first_next_step: Map the nearby papers by whether they treat employment, firm scale, or offshoring as the intermediate margin, then test which mechanism has the cleanest identification strategy.

wp:15532928__https://openalex.org/keywor.question_title: Does carbon trading move electricity prices through carbon costs, generation mix, or renewable standards?
wp:15532928__https://openalex.org/keywor.short_why: This is one of the cleanest climate mechanism cards in the set because the nearby channels are specific and already tied to market design.
wp:15532928__https://openalex.org/keywor.first_next_step: Start with power-market studies that jointly observe carbon pricing, wholesale prices, and the generation margin, then compare which intermediate channel carries the pass-through.

jel:O32:R&D__jel:E25:Wage.question_title: Does R&D raise wages through productivity gains, firm scale, or skill demand?
jel:O32:R&D__jel:E25:Wage.short_why: The local support graph suggests a classic but still usable wage mechanism question: research spending may matter only when it changes productivity, scale, or the demand for skilled labor.
jel:O32:R&D__jel:E25:Wage.first_next_step: Group the nearby papers by whether they emphasize productivity, firm growth, or skill composition, then test which channel best explains the wage response.

https://openalex.org/keywords/volatility.question_title: Does volatility change investor behavior through trading volume, announcements, or herding?
https://openalex.org/keywords/volatility.short_why: This is a strong finance card because the nearby channels are concrete market behaviors rather than generic sentiment language.
https://openalex.org/keywords/volatility.first_next_step: Start with event-level or market-microstructure papers that jointly track volatility and one candidate channel, then compare which channel predicts the behavioral response.

https://openalex.org/keywords/macroprude.question_title: Does macroprudential regulation curb leverage by reducing financial fragility?
https://openalex.org/keywords/macroprude.short_why: The graph keeps this question narrow: the main nearby route is balance-sheet fragility, which makes it more credible than a generic policy-to-leverage card.
https://openalex.org/keywords/macroprude.first_next_step: Start with papers that observe macroprudential tightening, leverage, and a fragility measure in the same setting, then test whether the leverage effect attenuates once fragility is accounted for.

jel:F20:Foreign Direct Investment__jel:Z.question_title: Does foreign direct investment support tourism development through infrastructure or sustainable tourism?
jel:F20:Foreign Direct Investment__jel:Z.short_why: This card survives because the route is specific enough to inspect: capital inflows may matter for tourism only when they improve infrastructure or shift the tourism mix.
jel:F20:Foreign Direct Investment__jel:Z.first_next_step: Start with papers that jointly cover FDI, tourism outcomes, and infrastructure or sustainability measures, then see which intermediate margin is actually observed.

https://openalex.org/keywords/fee-for-se.question_title: Does fee-for-service care change quality through payment design, managed-care substitution, or specialty mix?
https://openalex.org/keywords/fee-for-se.short_why: The nearby support is concrete enough to make this a usable health-economics mechanism card rather than a generic payment-quality debate.
https://openalex.org/keywords/fee-for-se.first_next_step: Separate papers by whether they study bundled-payment substitution, managed-care exposure, or specialty composition, then ask which channel changes measured quality.

jel:O32:R&D__jel:L25:Firm Size.question_title: Does R&D increase firm size through product innovation, patents, or firm growth?
jel:O32:R&D__jel:L25:Firm Size.short_why: This is a good mechanism card because the nearby literature points to a clear chain from research activity into innovation outputs and then scale.
jel:O32:R&D__jel:L25:Firm Size.first_next_step: Start with papers that measure R&D and at least one of product innovation, patenting, or firm growth, then test which intermediate margin best explains later size differences.

https://openalex.org/keywords/feed-in-ta.question_title: Do feed-in tariffs change investment timing through net present value, photovoltaic adoption, or real-option incentives?
https://openalex.org/keywords/feed-in-ta.short_why: This is one of the more focused climate-investment cards because the nearby channels are all about the investment margin rather than broad green-growth language.
https://openalex.org/keywords/feed-in-ta.first_next_step: Start with studies of feed-in tariffs and project timing, then compare whether NPV shifts, photovoltaic deployment, or explicit real-option reasoning does the explanatory work.

https://openalex.org/keywords/artificial.question_title: Can AI expand renewable energy through transition planning, labor adjustment, or deployment decisions?
https://openalex.org/keywords/artificial.short_why: The nearby support points to operational channels rather than a vague tech-to-green story: planning the transition, reallocating labor, and improving deployment decisions.
https://openalex.org/keywords/artificial.first_next_step: Start with papers that jointly study AI adoption and renewable deployment, then separate channels that operate through system planning, labor adjustment, or project siting and timing.

https://openalex.org/keywords/carbon-tax.question_title: Do carbon taxes expand wind power by shifting relative electricity prices?
https://openalex.org/keywords/carbon-tax.short_why: This is a narrow climate mechanism card. The nearby support says the route runs through electricity-price pass-through, not a broad green-growth narrative.
https://openalex.org/keywords/carbon-tax.first_next_step: Start with market-level studies that observe carbon taxes, power prices, and wind deployment in the same setting, then test whether price pass-through explains the investment response.

https://openalex.org/keywords/green-inno.question_title: Does green innovation raise productivity through R&D, firm upgrading, or labor reallocation?
https://openalex.org/keywords/green-inno.short_why: This card is useful because the local support points to concrete margins inside the firm rather than a generic claim that greener firms perform better.
https://openalex.org/keywords/green-inno.first_next_step: Start with firm-level studies that observe green innovation and at least one candidate channel, then test whether productivity gains survive once R&D, upgrading, or labor reallocation is measured directly.

jel:E43:Interest Rates__jel:E23:Output.question_title: Do interest rates move output through quantitative easing, monetary shocks, or fiscal imbalance?
jel:E43:Interest Rates__jel:E23:Output.short_why: The nearby graph suggests that the output response is not one mechanism. It passes through specific policy transmission channels that can be separated empirically.
jel:E43:Interest Rates__jel:E23:Output.first_next_step: Start with papers that jointly identify interest-rate changes and one candidate transmission channel, then compare whether the output effect attenuates once that channel is measured.

jel:G01:Global Financial Crisis__https:/.question_title: Did crisis exposure amplify volatility through contagion, emerging-market stress, or persistence?
jel:G01:Global Financial Crisis__https:/.short_why: The support graph points to a crisis-propagation question rather than a simple before-versus-after comparison of volatility.
jel:G01:Global Financial Crisis__https:/.first_next_step: Start with papers that track crisis exposure alongside volatility and one candidate channel, then compare whether contagion, emerging-market stress, or persistence best explains the response.

jel:G21:Lending__https://openalex.org/ke.question_title: Does lending feed systemic risk through bank balance sheets and commercial-bank exposure?
jel:G21:Lending__https://openalex.org/ke.short_why: This is a usable banking mechanism card because the nearby channels are balance-sheet objects, not abstract instability language.
jel:G21:Lending__https://openalex.org/ke.first_next_step: Start with papers that jointly study lending growth, systemic-risk measures, and bank balance-sheet conditions, then test whether the risk effect survives once bank exposure is accounted for.
